name: AI-enhanced Educational Game Design — Skills Crosswalk
description: Maps the 25 rubric criteria (5 deliverables × 5 criteria) to public skills taxonomies so that employers, registrars, and articulation agreements can reason about transferability. Each criterion resolves to at least one ESCO skill concept and one Lightcast Open Skills concept. Where a criterion is foundational to a broader competency framework (ISTE, UDL, NETP), we record that mapping as well. This file is read by alignment.html and by the OBv3 assertion pipeline.
_note_placeholders: Skill IRIs marked 'TBV' (To Be Verified) point to the taxonomy's public browser. A production deployment should pin each to a versioned concept URI; Lightcast skill IDs change across releases.

criteria.label: Learner specificity
criteria.description: Problem statement names a specific target learner group with age, prior experience, and context.

criteria.label: Context constraints
criteria.description: Classroom/time/device constraints surfaced explicitly and shaped the design space.

criteria.label: Measurable shift
criteria.description: Outcome stated as a measurable behavior change, not a topic to cover.

criteria.label: Evidence of the problem
criteria.description: Real data, artifacts, or observations justify why this is a problem worth solving.

criteria.label: Revision response
criteria.description: Peer/instructor feedback is tracked, triaged, and responded to with visible deltas.

criteria.label: Objective–mechanic traceability
criteria.description: Every game mechanic is traceable to at least one learning objective.

criteria.label: Mechanic fit
criteria.description: Chosen mechanic is appropriate to the cognitive demand of the objective.

criteria.label: Assessment alignment
criteria.description: Each objective has an in-game evidence source; no orphan assessments.

criteria.label: Cognitive load awareness
criteria.description: Extraneous load is named and managed; intrinsic load matches target learner.

criteria.label: Declined rows
criteria.description: At least one proposed objective or mechanic is declined with reasoning, showing scope discipline.

criteria.label: Core loop clarity
criteria.description: The playable core loop is identifiable in the prototype without narration.

criteria.label: Facilitator-runnable
criteria.description: A non-author can run the loop from the facilitator guide within target minutes.

criteria.label: Iteration log
criteria.description: Prototype iterations are logged with what changed and why.

criteria.label: Accessibility considered
criteria.description: Interaction modalities are examined against UDL multiple means.

criteria.label: Failure-state design
criteria.description: Loss/failure states are scaffolded for learning, not punitive.

criteria.label: Target-learner recruit
criteria.description: Playtesters match the target learner defined in D1.

criteria.label: Observation protocol
criteria.description: A structured observation protocol was followed and is reproducible.

criteria.label: Observation vs interpretation
criteria.description: Raw observations are separated from interpretations in the report.

criteria.label: Evidence-linked claims
criteria.description: Claims cite timestamped artifacts (video, notes, artifact photos).

criteria.label: Backlog discipline
criteria.description: Findings become backlog items with re-entry conditions, including declined items.

criteria.label: State-machine coherence
criteria.description: The implementation spec describes a coherent state machine, not a list of screens.

criteria.label: Event mapping
criteria.description: Learner-facing events are mapped to system-facing events (analytics + state).

criteria.label: D1–D4 citations
criteria.description: Every major spec decision cites a D1–D4 artifact as its warrant.

criteria.label: Known-limits annex
criteria.description: A Known Limits annex names what will not work and under what conditions.

criteria.label: Handoff-readiness
criteria.description: A technically trained non-author could begin implementation from the spec.
